code_value: Qian Zhongshu is one of the most famous Chinese writers. He was born on November 21, 1910 in Wuxi, Jiangsu Province. He lived with his uncle when he was very young. When he was a little boy, his uncle often took him to teahouses. He liked reading storybooks in his free time. In 1929, he went to Tsinghua University. Although he failed his math test, he entered the university because he was good at Chinese and English. In 1947, his most famous novel Fortress Besieged came out. It has been translated into more than six foreign languages. He died of illness on December 19, 1998. He was 88 years old then.
